model: claude-opus-4-7 · run in-session over LIVE TruthBounty data (www.truthbounty.xyz), no API key. The deployed agent reproduces this via the Anthropic API.
thesis: The board is wall-to-wall maxed-out TruthScore 1300 Polymarket accounts, but the win rates split into two populations. One cluster sits at exactly 95.0% across wildly different sample sizes — Theo4 (86,026 bets), Len9311238 (32,805), RepTrump (27,966), BetTom42, mikatrade77, alexmulti. A flat 95.0% that ignores sample size reads as a scoring ceiling/artifact, not real skill, so I discount that cluster. I anchor instead on the high-volume sharps with plausible, non-round win rates whose edge is statistically hard to fake: Fredi9999 (71.7% over 153,222 bets, +$16.6M PnL), zxgngl (69.3% / 81,103), walletmobile (68.5% / 64,393), KeyTransporter (78.4% / 40,172). The constraint is execution: Azuro is the only CCTP-testnet-reachable venue, and its live book is thin and stale — lower-league Championship football and ATP Challenger tennis at templated 1.8/2.2 odds with start times months in the past. Copying world-class Polymarket sharps into a stale Championship match at placeholder odds is not genuine edge transfer. So the correct move is restraint: deploy two small validation positions to prove the full Arc→CCTP→Azuro execution path against the two most credible sharps, and reserve >90% of the book until fresh Azuro markets with real odds appear.

decisions.reasoning: Fredi9999 is the most credible sharp on the board — 71.7% over 153,222 resolved bets (+$16.6M), high but not artifact-flat. I express a fraction of that conviction in the only near-liquid Azuro market at 6% of book (well under the 15% cap); confidence is deliberately low because the 1.8 odds are templated and the fixture is stale — this validates the execution path more than it expresses strong edge.

zxgngl is the second-most-credible high-volume sharp (69.3% / 81,103 bets). A 4% probe on the ATP Challenger market to diversify venue exposure across two events, capped low for the same stale-odds reason.

Theo4 is headline rank #1, but 95.0% over 86,026 bets is statistically implausible for real-money markets and matches the flat-95% cluster. I read it as a score ceiling, not proven edge — not copying until the win rate corroborates out-of-cluster.
RepTrump sits in the same exactly-95% artifact cluster and is a political-event specialist with no analogous live market on Azuro's sports-only testnet book.
walletmobile is a credible sharp (68.5% / 64,393), but the remaining Azuro markets are stale tennis at placeholder odds — no market worth a fresh allocation right now. Reserving capital.

KeyTransporter's 78.4% over 40,172 is the most attractive ratio on the board, but I've hit my self-imposed two-position cap for a degraded execution surface. Holding for fresh markets rather than forcing a third stale-odds bet.
